Title: Development of "Purple Endosperm Rice" by Engineering Anthocyanin Biosynthesis in the Endosperm with a High-Efficiency Transgene Stacking System

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses p-coumaroyl-CoA with malonyl-CoA to form naringenin chalcone
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: High transgene expression in developing endosperm (7 and 14 DAP) under endosperm-specific promoter
Pathway_Genes.Promoters: Endosperm-specific promoter, distinct ESP used in stacked construct
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances anthocyanin biosynthesis in rice endosperm; absence contributes to lighter purple grains and reduced anthocyanin content
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Multi-gene transgenic complementation in rice; semi-quantitative RT-PCR; PCR genotyping of partial-loss lines; HPLC metabolite profiling; antioxidant assay
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SsCHS was one of six structural genes introduced to reconstruct anthocyanin biosynthesis in rice endosperm. 2. It was highly expressed in developing endosperm of PER lines. 3. Lines lacking SsCHS together with SsCHI and SsF3'H showed lighter purple grains, indicating these structural transgenes enhanced anthocyanin accumulation beyond activation of endogenous counterparts.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Endogenous OsCHS could be activated by transgenic regulators, but the transgene still enhanced flux/output.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful as part of multigene stacks to build anthocyanin-rich cereal endosperm; should be combined with regulatory genes and downstream structural genes.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Public transcriptome data analysis (accession NA) for endogenous rice anthocyanin genes

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: High transgene expression in developing endosperm (7 and 14 DAP) under endosperm-specific promoter
Pathway_Genes.Promoters: Endosperm-specific promoter, distinct ESP used in stacked construct
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances anthocyanin biosynthesis in rice endosperm; absence contributes to lighter purple grains and reduced anthocyanin content
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Multi-gene transgenic complementation in rice; semi-quantitative RT-PCR; PCR genotyping of partial-loss lines; HPLC metabolite profiling; antioxidant assay
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SsCHI was stacked with other anthocyanin genes for endosperm engineering. 2. It was strongly expressed in PER developing endosperm. 3. Loss of SsCHI together with SsCHS and SsF3'H correlated with paler grains and reduced anthocyanin accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Endogenous OsCHI was activated, but transgenic SsCHI still contributed to higher pigmentation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful upstream structural component for anthocyanin biofortification stacks in cereals.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Public transcriptome data analysis (accession NA) for endogenous rice anthocyanin genes

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates flavanone to dihydroflavonol in the anthocyanin pathway
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: High transgene expression in developing endosperm (7 and 14 DAP)
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required to complete anthocyanin biosynthesis because endogenous OsF3H was not activated in endosperm
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic pathway reconstitution; semi-quantitative RT-PCR; comparison with endogenous gene expression; HPLC metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SsF3H was introduced because endogenous OsF3H was not expressed/activated in endosperm. 2. Its expression helped complete the reconstructed anthocyanin pathway. 3. It was identified as one of the necessary homologous structural genes complementing inactive endogenous capacity.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper explicitly states OsF3H was not activated by regulatory genes in both WT and PER, necessitating the transgene.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Critical complementation gene when endogenous F3H activity is absent in target tissue.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Introduces a 3'-hydroxyl group into flavonoid intermediates, enabling cyanidin-type anthocyanin formation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: High transgene expression in developing endosperm (7 and 14 DAP)
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances cyanidin/peonidin anthocyanin accumulation; absence contributes to lighter purple grains
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Multi-gene transgenic complementation; RT-PCR; PCR genotyping of lines missing gene; HPLC anthocyanin analysis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SsF3'H was included to channel flux toward cyanidin-derived anthocyanins. 2. It was highly expressed in developing PER endosperm. 3. Lines lacking SsF3'H together with SsCHS and SsCHI showed reduced pigmentation and anthocyanin content.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: End product profile dominated by C3G and methylated derivative P3G, consistent with cyanidin-branch engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important branch-specifying gene for cyanidin-type anthocyanin biofortification.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces dihydroflavonols to leucoanthocyanidins in the late anthocyanin pathway
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: High transgene expression in developing endosperm (7 and 14 DAP)
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for anthocyanin production because endogenous OsDFR is defective; absence abolishes purple seed production
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Sequence analysis of endogenous OsDFR defect; transgenic complementation; RT-PCR; PCR genotyping of line lacking SsDFR; grain color and HPLC phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsDFR is defective in the recipient rice varieties, so SsDFR was selected as a necessary complementing structural gene. 2. SsDFR was highly expressed in developing PER endosperm. 3. A line lacking SsDFR failed to produce purple seeds, demonstrating its essential role.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Strongest evidence among structural genes for necessity via missing-gene line H#12.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential replacement gene when endogenous DFR alleles are nonfunctional in white-endosperm rice backgrounds.

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: High transgene expression in developing endosperm (7 and 14 DAP)
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required to complete anthocyanin biosynthesis because endogenous OsANS was not activated in endosperm
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic pathway reconstitution; semi-quantitative RT-PCR; endogenous expression comparison; HPLC metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SsANS was introduced because endogenous OsANS was not activated in endosperm. 2. Its expression completed the late anthocyanidin-forming step in the engineered pathway. 3. Together with other structural genes, it enabled accumulation of C3G and P3G in polished rice endosperm.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper explicitly notes OsANS was not activated by regulatory genes in WT or PER, necessitating the transgene.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Critical late-pathway complementation gene for anthocyanin engineering in tissues lacking endogenous ANS expression.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Anthocyanin biosynthesis gene activation in endosperm
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Endogenous rice anthocyanin biosynthesis genes including OsCHS; OsCHI; OsF3'H; decorating and transporter genes; OsWD40
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates or enhances expression of 13 endogenous anthocyanin-related genes together with ZmPl and endogenous OsWD40
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms MBW complex with ZmPl and endogenous OsWD40
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential activator for engineered anthocyanin production in rice endosperm
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern: High transgene expression in developing endosperm (7 and 14 DAP)
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes anthocyanin accumulation in rice endosperm; required for purple grain formation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic expression in rice; semi-quantitative RT-PCR; analysis of lines missing ZmLc; HPLC anthocyanin profiling; grain color phenotype
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmLc was selected as the bHLH regulator to activate anthocyanin biosynthesis in rice endosperm. 2. Together with ZmPl and endogenous OsWD40, it rebuilt an MBW complex that activated/upregulated 13 endogenous anthocyanin-related genes. 3. Lines lacking only ZmLc produced white grains, demonstrating that ZmLc is required for the engineered pathway.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Endogenous OsB1/OsB2 are defective in recipient varieties; ZmLc functionally substitutes for missing/defective bHLH regulation.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Key regulatory gene for activating silent anthocyanin networks in cereal endosperm, best used with compatible MYB and structural-gene stack.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Most growth traits unchanged, but transgenic PER lines showed ~25% reduced grain weight overall; gene-specific attribution not resolved.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Public transcriptome data analysis (accession NA) for endogenous pathway expression

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Anthocyanin biosynthesis gene activation in endosperm
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Endogenous rice anthocyanin biosynthesis genes including OsCHS; OsCHI; OsF3'H; decorating and transporter genes; OsWD40
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates or enhances expression of 13 endogenous anthocyanin-related genes together with ZmLc and endogenous OsWD40
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms MBW complex with ZmLc and endogenous OsWD40
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Strong MYB activator used to induce anthocyanin biosynthesis; chosen because it has stronger activity than ZmC1
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern: High transgene expression in developing endosperm (7 and 14 DAP)
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes anthocyanin accumulation in rice endosperm as part of the rebuilt MBW regulator complex
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic expression in rice; semi-quantitative RT-PCR; endogenous target activation analysis; HPLC anthocyanin profiling; grain color phenotype
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmPl was selected as the MYB regulator because of stronger activity than ZmC1. 2. Together with ZmLc and OsWD40, it rebuilt the MBW complex in rice endosperm. 3. This regulatory module activated/upregulated 13 endogenous anthocyanin-related genes and supported anthocyanin accumulation in polished grains.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Endogenous OsC1 was not activated in PER; thus exogenous MYB activity remained necessary.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Valuable MYB regulator for anthocyanin biofortification in tissues where endogenous MYB regulators are silent or weak.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Most growth traits unchanged, but transgenic PER lines showed ~25% reduced grain weight overall; gene-specific attribution not resolved.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Public transcriptome data analysis (accession NA) for endogenous pathway expression
